claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits) and label-heavy structure indicate it uses a homophonic cipher with a reduced symbol set (fewer active glyphs per label), while the text-only section's high entropy (3.9016 bits) reflects unenciphered or lightly enciphered natural language prose â€” meaning these two sections require different decipherment strategies.
evidence_for: Zodiac entropy (3.7149) is lowest of all sections; text-only entropy (3.9016) is highest
Label registers in zodiac are typically short, repetitive, and positionally constrained
Text-only section entropy approaches Latin (~4.0) and Italian (~3.95) natural language levels
evidence_against: Section entropy differences are modest (~0.19 bits range), possibly explained by topic-specific vocabulary alone
A single cipher applied uniformly could still yield entropy variation through vocabulary domain effects
test: Compute bigram and trigram conditional entropy separately for zodiac vs. text-only sections; test whether zodiac shows significantly lower conditional entropy (indicating more repetitive/constrained sequences) and whether text-only conditional entropy matches that of a simple substitution cipher applied to Latin or Italian.
test_metric: Conditional bigram entropy H(g_n | g_{n-1}) per section; compare to expected values for Latin substitution cipher (~3.6â€“3.8 bits) and natural Latin prose (~3.5â€“3.7 bits)
test_threshold: If text-only conditional bigram entropy falls within 0.1 bits of Latin substitution cipher baseline and zodiac conditional entropy is >0.15 bits lower than text-only, the two-strategy hypothesis is supported

tests_run.test: compute bigram and trigram conditional entropy separately for zodiac vs. text-only sections; test wh
tests_run.details: Observed glyph entropy: 3.8627 bits. Expected for hypothesis: ~4.0 bits. Difference: 0.1373 bits.

reasoning: If confirmed, this would allow researchers to focus natural-language statistical attacks on the text-only section and label-matching attacks on the zodiac section independently, substantially narrowing the search space
